answer:
When Maia finds Arnold at the brook, he confesses his growing disillusionment with their marriage and his desire to live with Irena, claiming she alone holds the key to his artistic inspiration. Maia, though hurt, accepts his decision and even suggests the possibility of the three of them living together if she cannot find another place. Their conversation reveals Arnold’s emotional detachment and Maia’s reluctant acceptance of his choice.

\boxed{Maia discovers Arnold at the brook where he admits his unhappiness with their marriage and his intention to leave with Irena, whom he believes is essential to his artistic revival. Maia, though wounded, agrees to his decision and even proposes a tentative cohabitation with Irena. Their exchange underscores Arnold’s emotional detachment and Maia’s resigned acceptance of his choice.}
